output_summary: aligned schemas for datasets: kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, yelp_small; generated 2 schema correspondence groups.
profiled datasets: kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, yelp_small.
Reused the blocking configuration from state.

Blocking uses `semantic_similarity` for kaggle_small_uber_eats_small, uber_eats_small_yelp_small, blocking on name_norm, city, state; `exact_match_multi` for kaggle_small_yelp_small, blocking on phone_e164, postal_code.
Performance-wise, pair completeness is 1.00 for all pairs and candidate counts range from 179 to 199972.
The config also records dataset id columns.

matcher mode `rule_based` with config source reused from state; evaluated pairs: id_columns, matching_strategies; selected scores: matching_strategies (threshold=0.75, f1=0.8947368421052632, f1=0.983050847457627, threshold=0.72, f1=0.9302325581395349); produced 2 pair strategy entries.
This run created an initial draft of the pipeline.

The pipeline loads three parquet datasets, performs LLM-based schema matching to rename Uber Eats and Yelp columns into the Kaggle schema, and then runs pairwise entity matching across Kaggle-Uber Eats, Kaggle-Yelp, and Uber Eats-Yelp.

The pipeline uses heterogeneous blocking strategies, with embedding blocking for Kaggle-Uber Eats on name_norm, city, and state, standard blocking for Kaggle-Yelp on phone_e164 and postal_code, and embedding blocking for Uber Eats-Yelp on name_norm, street, and city.

The pipeline defines rule-based matching configurations with string and numeric comparators, custom weights, and thresholds of 0.75 for Kaggle-Uber Eats, 0.75 for Kaggle-Yelp, and 0.72 for Uber Eats-Yelp.

The fusion stage concatenates all pairwise correspondences and introduces attribute fusers for 19 fields, using longest_string for most text and location fields, union for categories, and maximum for rating and rating_count.
The run shifts to a rule-based multi-source matching and fusion workflow built from pairwise correspondences across all three datasets.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an initial draft of the evaluation script for the fused restaurant output.
The evaluation used ID-aligned comparison by constructing an evaluation view from fused clusters and matching each row to gold records through parsed _fusion_sources or fallback cluster IDs.
The strategy assigned tokenized_match to text and identifier-like fields, numeric_tolerance_match to numeric fields, and set_equality_match to categories and other detected list-like fields.
Name, website, URL, phone, and address-related fields were grouped under tokenized_match evaluation.
Latitude, longitude, rating, and rating_count were grouped under numeric_tolerance_match evaluation.
Categories and any additional detected list-like columns were grouped under set_equality_match evaluation.
The run added _parse_source_ids to decode _fusion_sources from JSON-like or Python-literal list representations.
The run added build_eval_view to expand fused cluster rows into eval_id-aligned rows, prefer higher _fusion_confidence matches, and deduplicate by eval_id.
The script falls back to using _id as eval_id when no source-based expansion is available.
The run added list-like column detection and normalization across fused and gold data before evaluation.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 66.15%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
The run is primarily failing in fusion logic and preprocessing rather than in blocking or matching.
The dominant issues are incorrect attribute conflict resolution, type and formatting mismatches, missing singleton output, and missing explicit handling for provenance and identifier fields.
Overall accuracy is 0.6615017878426699 and macro accuracy is 0.6772727272727272 across 40 evaluated records and 22 evaluated attributes.

Several key fused fields have catastrophic or very low accuracy, including phone_e164_accuracy of 0.0, phone_raw_accuracy of 0.0, house_number_accuracy of 0.0, postal_code_accuracy of 0.0, _id_accuracy of 0.275, source_accuracy of 0.275, rating_accuracy of 0.475, and map_url_accuracy of 0.4.
Debug evidence indicates systematic fusion mistakes because source is repeatedly fused as kaggle_380k when the expected source is yelp or uber_eats.
Debug evidence shows phone_e164 mismatches such as 16024284444.0 versus 16024284444, which is consistent with type formatting problems.
One gold record identified as kaggle380k-001820 has multiple missing_fused_value events with fused_id equal to NaN, which indicates a missing fused record rather than a comparison-only error.
This run was an evaluation-driven refinement of the existing pipeline.
The fusion layer was rewritten from broad longest-string defaults to attribute-specific fusers across 23 fields.

Phone fields now use canonical_phone, postal codes use canonical_postal_code, house numbers use canonical_house_number, latitude and longitude use canonical_latlon, and rating now uses canonical_rating instead of maximum.
Explicit fusers were added for id, _id, source, and kaggle380k_id using canonical_cluster_id and canonical_source.
Singleton retention was turned on by changing include_singletons to True.
Pre-fusion normalization was added for phone_e164, phone_raw, postal_code, house_number, name_norm, and multiple text fields, including removal of trailing .0 artifacts and null-string cleanup.
Fusion behavior shifted away from selecting the longest observed string toward consensus-style and canonicalized resolution.
Canonical business attributes such as name, address fields, street, city, state, country, website, and map_url now resolve with trusted_or_voting instead of longest_string.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an evaluation refinement rather than an initial draft or a repair.
The dominant strategy was to refine evaluation alignment and field-specific comparison coverage around the fused output rather than changing blocking or matching.
The run strengthened eval-id mapping by detecting the dominant gold ID prefix, preferring matching source IDs from `_fusion_sources`, and falling back to cluster `_id` only when needed.
Identity and provenance fields were brought into explicit evaluation coverage by adding tokenized evaluation for `source`, `_id`, and `kaggle380k_id`.

Textual business and address fields continued to use tokenized comparison across `name`, `name_norm`, `website`, `map_url`, `phone_raw`, `phone_e164`, `address_line1`, `address_line2`, `street`, `house_number`, `city`, `state`, `postal_code`, and `country`.
Numeric fields continued to use numeric tolerance matching for `latitude`, `longitude`, `rating`, and `rating_count`.
List-like fields were still normalized across fused and gold data and any detected non-underscore list-like columns were evaluated with set equality matching.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 51.61%; previous logged accuracy was 66.15%; evaluation attempt counter is 2.
The dominant failure in this run is fusion and evaluation alignment caused by incorrect ID and provenance handling in the fused output.
A large share of gold records are not recoverable in the evaluation view because fused rows are missing usable source ID mappings or singleton provenance.
The run also has systematic fusion logic errors for `_id` and `source`, plus weaker attribute-level fusion for phones, map URLs, ratings, and addresses.
Overall accuracy is 0.5160905840286055 and macro accuracy is 0.5197011579751517 across 40 evaluated records and 22 evaluated attributes.
Many debug mismatches are reported as `fused_id: NaN`, `inputs: []`, and `reason: "missing_fused_value"`.

Core fields share the same 0.725 accuracy, including `street`, `country`, `house_number`, `city`, `postal_code`, `state`, `latitude`, `longitude`, and `kaggle380k_id`, which indicates about 27.5% of gold records are not represented or mapped in the evaluation view.
`_id_accuracy` is 0.025 and `source_accuracy` is 0.025, showing that fused identity and source attribution are almost always wrong.
This run was a refinement in evaluation_refinement mode.

The pipeline replaced LLM-based schema matching for critical columns with deterministic schema alignment by requiring core fields, filling missing required columns, and explicitly propagating each dataset's id into kaggle380k_id.
The fusion logic changed identifier handling by replacing canonical_cluster_id with canonical_eval_id for kaggle380k_id, id, and _id.
The run split phone normalization and fusion into separate phone_raw and phone_e164 paths using normalize_phone_raw, normalize_phone_e164, canonical_phone_raw, and canonical_phone_e164.

Several attribute fusers were changed, including address_line1 to choose_longest_non_null, website and map_url to choose_first_non_null, and multiple text attributes such as name, street, city, state, country, and address_line2 to trusted_text.
Normalization was expanded to include URL cleanup, stronger name_norm cleanup, category parsing, and standardized preprocessing across core columns.
Fusion behavior shifted away from generic voting-style selection toward deterministic per-attribute canonicalization and first-non-null or trusted-text selection.
pipeline execution failed on attempt 1: error: Function resolver canonical_eval_id failed: canonical_eval_id() got an unexpected keyword argument 'sources'
Function resolver choose_longest_non_null failed: choose_longest_non_null() got an unexpected keyword argument 'sources'
Fun; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an execution repair that reworked the pipeline after the previous failure caused by fusion resolver functions not accepting keyword arguments like 'sources'.

The repair updated custom fusion helper functions such as canonical_eval_id, choose_longest_non_null, choose_first_non_null, trusted_text, canonical_name_norm, canonical_phone_raw, canonical_phone_e164, canonical_postal_code, canonical_house_number, canonical_rating, canonical_latlon, canonical_source, and related utilities to accept extra keyword arguments via '**kwargs'.
The run introduced a shared is_nullish helper and refactored normalization and selection helpers to use it for more consistent null handling across text, phones, categories, and numeric parsing.
The fused identifier behavior remained configured to use canonical_eval_id for kaggle380k_id, id, and _id, while source continued to use canonical_source and singleton retention stayed enabled.
The immediate strategy shift was operational rather than conceptual, changing fusion helper signatures and null handling so the configured fusion strategy could execute without resolver errors.
pipeline execution failed on attempt 2: error: Function resolver canonical_eval_id failed: too many values to unpack (expected 3)
Function resolver choose_longest_non_null failed: too many values to unpack (expected 3)
Function resolver trusted_text failed: too many values to unp; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an execution repair focused on fixing fusion function failures from the previous attempt.

The repair added a shared value-flattening helper and updated custom fuser functions such as choose_first_non_null, choose_longest_non_null, trusted_text, canonical_name_norm, canonical_phone_e164, canonical_phone_raw, canonical_postal_code, canonical_house_number, canonical_rating, canonical_latlon, canonical_source, and canonical_eval_id to consume flattened inputs safely.
The null handling logic was broadened so is_nullish now tolerates additional empty container-like values such as [] and {} and safely wraps pd.isna checks.

The pipeline kept deterministic schema alignment and normalization for critical columns, while preserving the same blocking, matcher, singleton inclusion, and attribute-fuser assignments shown in this run.

The fusion behavior shifted from brittle resolver signatures that crashed on incoming value shapes to shape-robust fusers that normalize scalar, list, numpy array, and pandas Series inputs before selection.
The matching and blocking strategy did not materially change in this run, and the repair was concentrated on making fusion execution resilient rather than changing correspondence generation logic.
pipeline execution failed on attempt 3: error: Function resolver canonical_eval_id failed: too many values to unpack (expected 3)
Function resolver choose_longest_non_null failed: too many values to unpack (expected 3)
Function resolver trusted_text failed: too many values to unp; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an execution repair focused on fixing fusion function resolver failures in the pipeline.
The run removed the `_id` attribute fuser `canonical_eval_id` from the fusion configuration.
The run changed the `id` fuser from `canonical_eval_id` to `choose_first_non_null`.
The run changed the `kaggle380k_id` fuser from `canonical_eval_id` to `choose_first_non_null`.
The run changed the `source` fuser from `canonical_source` to `choose_first_non_null`.

The helper fuser functions were rewritten to simpler signatures without `**kwargs`, including `choose_first_non_null`, `choose_longest_non_null`, `trusted_text`, `canonical_name_norm`, `canonical_phone_e164`, `canonical_phone_raw`, `canonical_postal_code`, `canonical_house_number`, `canonical_rating`, and `canonical_latlon`.
Fusion behavior shifted away from canonical or priority-based identifier and source selection toward first-non-null selection for `id`, `kaggle380k_id`, and `source`.
The run reduced special handling of fused identity by removing explicit `_id` fusion while retaining `_id` as an alignment field.
The repair prioritized resolver compatibility and successful execution over provenance-aware or cluster-based ID fusion.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 4; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an evaluation refinement focused on adjusting the evaluation script rather than drafting a new evaluation pipeline.
The refinement updated evaluation-view construction, coverage diagnostics, and metric reporting based on the latest fusion mismatches and score patterns.
The dominant strategy was to improve evaluation alignment by rebuilding how fused rows are mapped to gold IDs through provenance-aware eval_id selection.

The run emphasized evaluation-side ID coverage and candidate selection logic, while continuing to use tokenized matching for text fields, numeric tolerance matching for numeric fields, and set equality for detected list-like columns.

Textual entity and address-family fields were evaluated with tokenized matching, including name, name_norm, website, map_url, phone_raw, phone_e164, address_line1, address_line2, street, house_number, city, state, postal_code, country, source, and kaggle380k_id.
Numeric fields were grouped under tolerance-based evaluation, including latitude, longitude, rating, and rating_count.
Detected list-like non-underscore columns were grouped under set-equality evaluation after shared normalization across fused and gold dataframes.
